pattern: A bulb ends on only if it was toggled an odd number of times, which happens exactly for positions with an odd count of divisors, the perfect squares, so the answer is the integer square root of n.
hints: A bulb at position k is toggled once for every divisor of k, so its final state depends on whether k has an odd or even number of divisors.
Divisors pair up except when the position is a perfect square, where one divisor is its own partner.
Count how many perfect squares are at most n; that is just the integer part of the square root of n.

edge_inputs.description: floating-point sqrt must not round up here

stress.description: random n including values just around perfect squares
stress.note: stresses the boundary where sqrt rounding errors would bite
